div.head:
No. 412.
Mr. Moran to Mr. Evarts.

opener.dateline:
Legation of the United States,
Lisbon, May 1, 1879. (Received May 19.)

div.p:
Sir: On the receipt of your No. 134, I addressed a note in the sense thereof to Mr. Corvo, a copy of which I inclose, expressing the satisfaction of the Government of the United States with the action of the governor-general of the Cape Verde Islands in providing a new cemetery for the remains of the Americans now lying in the old naval cemetery
there, and requested that information of these sentiments of his kindness may be officially communicated to the governor-general in question.
My note was addressed to Mr. Corvo on the 4th of March. But should one come I will immediately forward a copy.
